__warnings.note: Kept Shona 'Mhoro' verbatim — it's Rwen's brand-signature greeting and recognised across the app. Reviewer: confirm whether Japanese speakers should keep 'Mhoro' or flip to 'こんにちは、{{name}}さん！' for clarity with early users.
Kept Shona 'Ndine urombo' (I'm sorry) verbatim for brand-signature consistency. Japanese explanation follows. Reviewer: confirm or flip to 'すみません —'.
Same Mhoro decision applied. Reviewer: confirm.
'{{lang}} companion · AI' rendered as '{{lang}}コンパニオン · AI'. The {{lang}} placeholder may render the target language name in Japanese (e.g. ショナ語) or English depending on i18n wiring. Verify upstream.

Used polite-form (です／ます) throughout for the UI chrome. Rwen's persona prompt also defaults to polite-form but is allowed to soften per user. Reviewer: decide whether the entire pack should switch to plain-form (だ／である) for a more friendly companion feel, or stay polite-form for consistency with Japanese app convention.

Used '{{name}}さん' as the courteous default. Reviewer: confirm — for very casual companions, dropping the suffix or using 'ちゃん'/'くん' may feel warmer, but suffix decisions in Japanese are sensitive and should be user-research-backed.

banner.speak_now: 🎤 どうぞ話してください…

input.placeholder_convo_active: 会話中 — 話すか、⏹をタップして止める

messages.welcome: Mhoro、{{name}}さん！今日はどんなことが気になっていますか？
messages.error_generic: Ndine urombo — 何か問題が発生しました。もう一度お試しください。
messages.auto_listen_greeting: Mhoro！聞いていますよ。準備ができたら、いつでも話しかけてください。
